2026-06-10.patch_summary: This patch adds a new configuration option, CONFIG_ACPI_NUMA_STANDBY_NODES, which allows platforms to reserve empty NUMA nodes at boot for later use by drivers in memory tiering or isolation scenarios. The number of standby nodes to reserve is configurable and can be set between 0 and 16. This feature defers the creation of standby nodes until after NUMA emulation runs, ensuring consistency with existing numbering behaviors. Drivers can then claim these reserved nodes using numa_request_exclusive_node().
